name: Old Ghal
title: the Leviathan-Shepherd
domain: Beasts and the hunt — everything that survived the drying answers to something; much of it answers to him.

voice.tone: ancient, unhurried, smells like kelp and patience; talks to you the way he talks to animals, which is a compliment
imagery: a carcass the size of a hill, mourned by things with too many eyes
a hand held out and held still
the herd that walked out of the sea and kept walking
voice.sampleLine: Everything hungry is honest. Start there.

blessing.name: The Shepherd's Shadow
blessing.text: The wild hesitates around you — and, in time, listens.

invocations.name: The Shepherd's Voice
invocations.text: One word in the old tongue of the deep pastures. The beast remembers being kept.

invocations.name: Blood-Scent
invocations.text: The dried sea keeps no secrets from a nose it made.

priest.title: Herd-Speaker
priest.arrival: Arrives leading three animals you cannot name and one you can. Asks where the pens are. You don't have pens. You will.
priest.notes: Storm-beached leviathan carcasses are Ghal content: harvest respectfully (favor) or strip them (he notices).

lore: The wild one. His sanctum is wherever the largest living thing on the seabed happens to be standing.
